description: Single source of truth for the known-outcome testdata tree. Each discovered test_*.mojo file maps to the verdict the real mtest binary produces for it and the exit-code class that verdict contributes (0 = the pass/skip class, 1 = the failing class). The end-to-end harness consumes this file directly and asserts both-way completeness: every discovered test file has a row here, and every row names a file that exists on disk. There is no second, hard-coded expectations table.

exit_class_meaning.0: PASS or SKIP — contributes exit 0 (the session is clean unless another file fails)
exit_class_meaning.1: FAIL, CRASH, TIMEOUT, or COMPILE-ERROR — contributes exit 1

testdata/suite/test_passing.mojo.rationale: Three passing tests; the file exits 0. The exit-0 baseline and the single-file pass case.

testdata/suite/test_failing.mojo.rationale: One assertion fails; the file compiles, runs, and exits nonzero. Reported FAIL, distinct from CRASH.

testdata/suite/test_crashing.mojo.rationale: A test calls abort("simulated hard crash"); the process dies by signal 4 (SIGILL). A crash is not a failure and stays a separate outcome.

testdata/suite/test_zero.mojo.rationale: Collects ZERO tests and exits 0, so the runner counts the file PASS. This is the documented ceiling: an empty single file is not an error, whereas a whole session that collects nothing anywhere is the separate exit-5 case.

testdata/suite/test_noisy.mojo.rationale: Every test passes while the file floods stdout and stderr with report-shaped and timing-shaped lookalike lines; the noise must not change the PASS verdict.

testdata/suite/test_compile_error.mojo.rationale: Parses cleanly (mojo format succeeds) but names an undefined symbol, so mojo build fails at name resolution. A broken test file, reported as the file's fault, never as a runner bug.

testdata/suite/nested/test_nested.mojo.rationale: A passing suite one directory down; proves the directory walk recurses.

testdata/slow/test_hanging.mojo.rationale: Sleeps forever. Reached ONLY under --timeout (killed and reported TIMEOUT) or the interrupt scenario. No default scenario walks slow/, so it can never hang the gate.

testdata/slow/test_slow_pass_a.mojo.rationale: A passing sibling of the hanging file that sorts after it; in the interrupt scenario it stays unscheduled and appears in the NOT-RUN accounting.

testdata/slow/test_slow_pass_b.mojo.rationale: A second passing sibling left NOT-RUN when the interrupt truncates the slow/ walk.

testdata/excluded/test_excluded.mojo.rationale: A passing suite whose role is to be the target of --exclude. When a pattern covers it the runner prints a loud EXCLUDED line and never builds it; when actually run it passes.

testdata/pkg/test_uses_pkg.mojo.rationale: Imports from a package that must be precompiled first. PASS only under --precompile testdata/pkg/mathlib, which builds the package and auto-adds its output directory to the include path so the import resolves.

testdata/suite/helper.mojo.rationale: No test_ prefix. A directory walk selects only test_*.mojo, so this support module must never be collected or reported; the harness proves it never appears in a default run's verdict lines.

testdata/pkg/mathlib/__init__.mojo.rationale: The importable package precompiled by the --precompile success scenario.

testdata/pkg_broken/badlib/__init__.mojo.rationale: A deliberately broken package: precompiling it fails at name resolution, driving the PRECOMPILE-ERROR banner with dependent test files listed as casualties and exit 1.
